what_it_does: A posted note by Tim in the dragnet-development channel, replying to board item item-d60bd0c6, that affirms prior findings and reframes the gap-list build as three concrete, scoped pieces: a parser, a recursive read, and a node-type vocabulary.
observations: YAML frontmatter follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
The body is a short paragraph reply that quotes a prior verdict and expresses agreement.
It links to another board item via a commented_on relationship.
Created and updated timestamps are identical, with a single history entry showing a filed/posted transition.
standouts: Reframes a potentially open-ended task into three named, concrete build pieces.
Functions as social/feedback signal rather than a specification or code artifact.
Uses a structured board link to thread the reply.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item format with board:// URI addressing.
Markdown body prefixed with a [paragraph] marker.
Includes a history transition from null to posted.
concerns: The note references item-d60bd0c6 without quoting enough of its content, so full meaning depends on external context.
notes: Tim's brief affirmative reply to a prior board item, validating a scoped breakdown of the gap-list work.
questions: What is the 'gap-list' and what was the full content of the verdict in item-d60bd0c6?
What are the specific parser, recursive read, and node-type vocabulary implementations under discussion?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose as a note/paragraph comment matches the actual content: a short appreciative reply to another board item.
intention_signals: To acknowledge and reinforce a teammate's analysis, agreeing that the gap-list edge exists and that the required build is smaller and more scoped than building a document model from scratch.
contribution: Captures a concise human endorsement and reframing of technical findings, preserving the social context of agreement and scoping alongside structured board metadata.
